ogTitle: Wallabi Point vs Wallacetown: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wallabi Point and Wallacetown.
heading: Wallabi Point vs Wallacetown — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wallabi Point, New South Wales and Wallacetown, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wallacetown has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Wallabi Point (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wallabi Point or Wallacetown safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wallabi Point has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Wallacetown scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wallabi Point scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Wallacetown scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wallabi Point or Wallacetown?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wallacetown has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
